rules.rule: Brokers MUST be the only layer that talks to external dependencies (APIs, storage, logging, external services).
Broker methods MUST use async/await for all I/O operations.
Brokers MUST map external exceptions to broker-layer exceptions with meaningful messages.
API broker methods MUST use standard HTTP verbs: Get, Post, Put, Delete.
Broker method names MUST follow pattern: `{verb}{Entity}Async` (e.g., `getPatientAsync`, `postPatientAsync`).
Storage brokers MUST abstract localStorage/sessionStorage/IndexedDB behind interface.
Logging brokers MUST abstract console or external logging services.
Brokers MUST NOT contain business logic (filtering, validation, transformation beyond format conversion).
Brokers MUST be injected via dependency injection, not instantiated directly in services.
DateTime brokers MUST abstract Date object creation and manipulation.
Brokers MUST be organized by external dependency type: apis/, storages/, loggings/, datetimes/.
Broker class names MUST follow pattern: `{Entity}{Type}Broker` (e.g., `PatientApiBroker`, `LocalStorageBroker`).
Each broker MUST serve one and only one external dependency.
Brokers SHOULD expose interfaces for testing and mocking.
Brokers MUST catch external exceptions and wrap them in broker-specific exceptions.
Broker exceptions MUST preserve the original exception as inner exception.
Brokers MUST NOT catch and swallow exceptions without rethrowing.
Brokers MUST NOT return error codes — use exceptions for error signaling.
Brokers MUST NOT call other brokers — composition happens in services.
Brokers MUST NOT perform retries or circuit breaking — that belongs in services.
API brokers SHOULD accept CancellationToken-equivalent (AbortSignal) for request cancellation.
